pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: KO offers a defensive, dividend-reliable franchise trading at a moderate valuation (P/E 25.0, near the low end of its 23.2–30.0 range) with improving leverage and steady organic growth guidance of 4–5%. However, volatile quarterly earnings, unverifiable cash-flow data, and a CEO transition introduce enough uncertainty to limit conviction to a mild positive tilt rather than a confident long.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: KO offers a defensive, dividend-reliable franchise trading at a moderate valuation (P/E 25.0, near the low end of its 23.2–30.0 range) with improving leverage and steady organic growth guidance of 4–5%. However, volatile quarterly earnings, unverifiable cash-flow data, and a CEO transition introduce enough uncertainty to limit conviction to a mild positive tilt rather than a confident long.
key_factors: Valuation at lower end of range: P/E of 25.0 as of 2026-03-31 sits near the bottom of the one-year 23.2–30.0 range, with P/B of 9.57 vs. a 9.4–12.1 range, offering modest but not compelling support.
Improving balance-sheet leverage: year-end debt-to-equity declined from ~2.81x (2024-12-31) to ~2.06x (2025-12-31), indicating meaningful deleveraging despite interim data anomalies.

Steady but unspectacular growth outlook: 2026 guidance calls for 4–5% organic revenue growth and 5–6% comparable currency-neutral EPS growth, with 2025 having delivered 5% organic revenue growth and 120 bps of adjusted operating margin expansion to 31.2%.

Earnings volatility and data gaps: quarterly EPS ranged from $0.51 to $0.88 over the available series, and the absence of cash-flow data and missing Q3/Q4 2025 income statements prevent a full earnings-quality assessment, tempering conviction.

es and accessories. Therefore, many components used by the Company, including those that are available from multiple sources, are at times subject to industry-wide shortage and significant commodity pricing fluctuations. Restrictions on international trade can increase the cost or limit the availability of the Company's products and the components and rare earths and other raw materials that go into them.
The Company uses some custom components that are not commonly used by its competitors, and new products introduced by the Company often utilize custom components available from only one source. When a component or product uses new technologies, initial capacity constraints may exist until the suppliers' yields have matured or their manufacturing capacities have increased. The Company has entered into agreements for the supply of many components; however, the Company may not be able to extend or renew agreements for the supply of components on similar terms, or at all, and may not be successful in obtaining sufficient quantities from its suppliers or in a timely manner, or in identifying and obtaining sufficient quantities from an alternative source. In addition, component suppliers may fail, be subject to consolidation within a particular industry, or decide to concentrate on the production of common components instead of components customized to meet the Company's requirements, further limiting the Company's ability to obtain sufficient quantities of components on commercially reasonable terms, or at all.
Research and Development
Because the industries in which the Company competes are characterized by rapid technological advances, the Company's ability to compete successfully depends heavily upon its ability to ensure a continual and timely flow of competitive products, services and technologies to the marketplace. The Company continues to develop new technologies to enhance existing products and services, and to expand the range of its offerings through research and development ("R&D"), licensing of intellectual property and acquisition of third-party businesses and technology.
Intellectual Property
The Company currently holds a broad collection of intellectual property rights relating to certain aspects of its hardware, software and services. This includes patents, designs, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets and other forms of intellectual property rights in the U.S. and various foreign countries. Although the Company believes the ownership of such intellectual property rights is an important factor in differentiating its business and that its success does depend in part on such ownership, the Company relies primarily on the innovative skills, technical competence and marketing abilities of its personnel.
The Company regularly files patent, design, copyright and trademark applications to protect innovations arising from its hardware, software and service research, development, design and marketing, and is currently pursuing thousands of applications around the world. Over time, the Company has accumulated a large portfolio of issued and registered intellectual property rights around the world. No single intellectual property right is solely responsible for protecting the Company's products and services. The Company believes the duration of its intellectual property rights is adequate relative to the expected lives of its products and services.
Apple Inc. | 2025 Form 10-K | 3
In addition to Company-owned intellectual property, many of the Company's products and services include technology or intellectual property that must be licensed from third parties. It may be necessary in the future to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of the Company's products, processes and services. While the Company has generally been able to obtain such licenses on commercially reasonable terms in the past, there is no guarantee that such licenses could be obtained in the future on reasonable terms or at all.
Business Seasonality and Product Introductions
The Company has historically experienced higher net sales in its first quarter compared to other quarters in its fiscal year due in part to seasonal holiday demand. Additionally, new product and service introductions can significantly impact net sales, cost of sales and operating expenses. The timing of product introductions can also impact the Company's net sales to its indirect distribution channels as these channels are filled with new inventory following a product launch, and channel inventory of an older product often declines as the launch of a newer product approaches. Net sales can also be affected when consumers and distributors anticipate a product introduction.
Human Capital
The Company believes that its people play an important role in its success, and strives to attract, develop and retain the best talent. The Company works to create a culture of collaboration, one where people with a broad range of backgrounds and perspectives can come together to innovate and do the best work of their lives. The Company is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and to providing a workplace free of harassment or discrimination. As of September 27, 2025, the Company had approximately 166,000 full-time equivalent employees.
The Company believes that compensation should be competitive and equitable, and offers discretionary cash and equity awards to enable employees to share in the Company's success. The Company recognizes its people are most likely to thrive when they have the resources to meet their needs and the time and support to succeed in their professional and personal lives. In support of this, the Company offers a wide variety of benefits for employees around the world, including health, wellness and time away.
The Company invests in resources to help its people develop and achieve their career goals. The Company offers programs through Apple University on leadership, management and influence, as well as Apple culture and values. Team members can also take advantage of online classes for business, technical and personal development.
The Company believes that open and honest communication among team members, managers and leaders helps create an open, collaborative work environment where everyone can contribute, grow and succeed. Team members are encouraged to come to their managers with questions, feedback or concerns, and the Company conducts surveys that gauge employee sentiment in areas like career development, manager performance and inclusion.
The Company is committed to the safety and security of its team members everywhere it operates. The Company supports employees with general safety, security and crisis management training, and by putting specific programs in place for those working in potentially high-hazard environments.
Available Information
The Company's Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K, and amendments to reports filed pursuant to Sections 13(a) and 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended ("Exchange Act"), are filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"). Such reports and other information filed by the Company with the SEC are available free of charge at investor.apple.com/investor-relations/sec-filings/default.aspx when such reports are available on the SEC's website. The Company periodically provides certain information for investors on its corporate website, www.apple.com, and its investor relations website, investor.apple.com. This includes press releases and other information about financial performance, information on corporate governance, and details related to the Company's annual meeting of shareholders. The information contained on the websites referenced in this Form 10-K is not incorporated by reference into this filing. Further, the Company's references to website URLs are intended to be inactive textual references only.
Apple Inc. | 2025 Form 10-K | 4
AAPL.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: AAPL (2026-04-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Strong sequential and annual growth. Latest Q1 2025 (period end 2025-12-27) revenue of $143.76B represents a +40.3% QoQ increase and a +15.7% YoY increase. The TTM revenue is $435.62B.
*   **Earnings & Margins:** Trajectory aligns with revenue. Q1 2025 net income was $42.10B (YoY +15.9%) with EPS of $2.84 (YoY +18.3%). Margins are stable: latest gross margin is 47.3%, operating margin is 32.4%, and net margin is 27.0%.
*   **Cash-Flow:** *No quarterly cash flow data is supplied.*
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity has increased from $56.95B (Q4 2024) to $88.19B (Q1 2025). Total debt has decreased steadily from $102.60B (Q2 2024) to $88.50B (Q1 2025).

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** High reported growth (EPS +18.3% YoY) is supported by consistent margin expansion (gross margin +480 bps YoY from 42.5% to 47.3% per Q1 2025 data). The ROE is extremely high at 1.335 (133.5%), indicating efficient equity utilization, though this is amplified by the leveraged capital structure.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The debt-to-equity ratio is 1.004. While not excessive, the company carries more debt ($88.50B) than equity ($88.19B). The trend is positive, with debt reduced by $14.10B over four quarters. Asset growth ($379.30B) has outpaced liability growth ($291.11B).

**3. Valuation vs. Own History**
*   Latest PE ratio is 31.9x, at the lower end of its 1-year range of 27.2x to 40.9x.
*   Latest Price-to-Book is 42.6x, also near the low end of its 1-year range of 39.1x to 61.6x.
*   Valuation multiples have contracted from recent highs (PE was 37.5x in October 2025), despite improving net margins (from 0.243 to 0.270).

**4. Price Level and Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
*   **Price:** Latest close is $253.79, down 7.3% over the last quarter but up 16.5% over the last year. The stock has traded in a range of $169.21 to $288.62.
*   **Context:** The recent quarterly price decline (-7.3%) occurs against a backdrop of strong fundamental improvement (Q1 2025 revenue +15.7% YoY, EPS +18.3% YoY). The stock's 1-year gain of +16.5% lags the TTM earnings growth of +18.3%.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** Continued revenue growth momentum (+15.7% YoY) coupled with stable high margins (gross margin 47.3%, operating margin 32.4%) could drive further earnings expansion. The declining debt load is a positive balance sheet trend.
*   **Downside:** The valuation remains elevated (PE of 31.9x) relative to history and fundamentals. The extreme ROE (1.335) is a function of leverage (D/E of 1.004), which introduces risk. The lack of cash flow data is an assessment gap.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
*   **Conclusion:** The quantitative profile shows a company with strong, improving fundamentals (robust revenue growth, expanding margins, strengthening balance sheet) that is currently trading at a valuation that, while off its highs, remains historically elevated. The price has corrected recently (-7.3% QoQ) despite positive earnings momentum, creating a modest divergence. The risk is that the valuation multiple continues to compress even as fundamentals hold. The reward hinges on the durability of the growth and margin trajectory to support the current multiple. The balance sheet is leveraged but de-risking. Overall, the numbers present a profile of solid fundamental quality priced at a premium, with near-term price action having moved slightly ahead of the fundamental improvement rate.
AAPL.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Apple Inc. (AAPL)**
**Decision Date:** 2026-04-01
**Evidence Window:** Through 2026-03-31

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on the 2025 Form 10-K (filing_date=2025-10-31), Apple designs, manufactures, and markets a broad ecosystem of smartphones (iPhone), personal computers (Mac), tablets (iPad), wearables (Apple Watch, AirPods, Vision Pro), and accessories, supported by a high-margin services segment. Services include advertising, AppleCare, cloud services, digital content and subscriptions (App Store, Apple Music, TV+), and payment services (Apple Pay). The business is managed on a geographic basis with segments for Americas, Europe, Greater China, Japan, and Rest of Asia Pacific. The franchise is characterized by tight integration of hardware, software, and services, a large installed base, and strong brand loyalty, though the 10-K notes it holds a minority market share in its primary hardware categories.

**2. Recent Developments (2026-02-10 to 2026-03-31)**
*   **AI Strategy:** As of 2026-03-31, Apple is taking a partnership approach to AI, notably by integrating Google's Gemini into Siri, rather than building its own large-scale LLM. This is viewed as leveraging its device installed base and avoiding massive capital expenditure.
*   **Capital Allocation & Investor Sentiment:** Warren Buffett, in a statement dated 2026-03-31, confirmed Apple remains Berkshire Hathaway's largest holding (23% of portfolio) and expressed he would buy "a whole lot" more if the stock price declined, praising Tim Cook's leadership.
*   **Regulatory & Legal:** As of 2026-03-30 and 2026-02-25, Apple faces a major U.S. antitrust lawsuit alleging monopolistic practices with iPhone and iOS, and ongoing regulatory pressure in Europe under the Digital Markets Act. Separately, a 2026-02-13 article notes a $4 billion legal battle over Apple Pay.
*   **Market Context:** A 2026-03-31 article identifies Apple as a "Magnificent Seven" stock that is lagging amid a broader rotation into value and defensive sectors, with tech indices under pressure.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Regulatory & Antitrust:** Confirmed ongoing U.S. and EU legal actions (2026-03-30, 2026-02-25) could force changes to App Store fees, iOS ecosystem rules, or business practices, potentially impacting Services revenue and installed base control.
    *   **AI Execution & Competition:** The partnership-centric AI strategy (2026-03-31) may be perceived as ceding leadership to rivals investing heavily in proprietary models, potentially impacting long-term ecosystem stickiness.
    *   **Geopolitical & Supply Chain:** The 10-K (2025-10-31) explicitly cites risks from single-source components, trade restrictions, and geopolitical tensions affecting rare earth materials and manufacturing.
    *   **Market Sentiment:** Evidence from 2026-03-31 indicates a broader market shift away from growth and tech stocks, which could pressure Apple's valuation multiple.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Installed Base & Ecosystem:** The 10-K and 2026-03-31 articles emphasize the vast installed base (2.5B devices) and integrated ecosystem as key moats, facilitating service adoption and user retention.
    *   **Cash Flow & Investor Confidence:** Warren Buffett's stated willingness to buy on weakness (2026-03-31) signals strong fundamental conviction from a major, long-term investor.
    *   **Capital-Light AI Model:** By partnering for core AI, Apple may achieve rapid feature integration at lower relative R&D and capital costs (2026-03-31).
    *   **Product Diversification:** The broad product portfolio across consumer electronics and high-growth services provides multiple revenue streams.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** The franchise remains fundamentally strong with a durable ecosystem and committed long-term holders. However, significant regulatory headwinds and questions about AI leadership in the current cycle create material uncertainty. Recent market dynamics are negative for the stock, though the core business model appears resilient. The near-term outlook is balanced between stable product/service demand and legal/competitive overhangs.

---
*Confirmed evidence is drawn exclusively from the supplied 10-K and dated news snippets. Inference is limited to linking documented facts (e.g., regulatory action and ecosystem strength).*

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'gross margin +480 bps YoY from 42.5% to 47.3% per Q1 2025 data.' The raw evidence shows gross_margin=0.473 for the latest period, but the YoY comparison figure of 42.5% is not directly supplied in the raw packs. The raw income statement shows Q1 2024 (2024-12-28) gross profit of $58.27B on revenue of $124.30B, which yields ~46.9%, not 42.5%. The 42.5% figure appears to be unsupported or sourced from outside the pack. The actual YoY gross margin expansion appears to be roughly +40 bps, not +480 bps.
corrections.required_action: Correct the YoY gross margin comparison. The 42.5% figure is not evidenced in the supplied data; recalculating from the raw income statement gives ~46.9% for Q1 FY2024 vs 47.3% for Q1 FY2025, a ~40 bps expansion, not 480 bps.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'improving net margins (from 0.243 to 0.270)' referencing valuation history. The raw valuation sparse history shows net_margin=0.243 from 2025-04-01 through 2025-10-15, then 0.269 from 2025-11-17 through 2026-01-26, then 0.270 from 2026-02-26 onward. This is consistent with the raw data. However, the quant report's claim of 'margin expansion' as a driver of earnings quality should note that the net margin jump from 0.243 to 0.269/0.270 coincides with the Q1 2025 (Dec 2025) earnings release entering the TTM calculation, replacing the low-EPS Q4 FY2024 quarter (eps=0.97). This is a mechanical rolling effect, not necessarily organic margin improvement, and should be flagged for earnings quality assessment.
corrections.required_action: Add context that the net margin improvement from 0.243 to 0.270 is largely a mechanical effect of the low-Q4 FY2024 quarter (eps=0.97) rolling out of the TTM window, rather than evidence of organic margin expansion.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'Apple will pay $1…' in the AI strategy section, referencing the 2026-03-31 article about Apple's partnership with Google Gemini. The raw evidence summary for that article says 'Apple will pay $1…' but the sentence is truncated in the source. The qual report does not complete this figure. This is a minor completeness transparency issue but does not materially affect the assessment.
corrections.required_action: No action required; truncated reference is noted but does not change the qualitative conclusion.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Apple's fundamentals remain strong—revenue growing +15.7% YoY, stable margins, and a de-risking balance sheet—but the stock trades at a historically elevated P/E of 31.9x amid material regulatorytailwinds from antitrust litigation and questions about AI strategy leadership. The recent -7.3% quarterly price decline has modestly improved the risk/reward, but not enough to warrant a clear directional call.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Apple's fundamentals remain strong—revenue growing +15.7% YoY, stable margins, and a de-risking balance sheet—but the stock trades at a historically elevated P/E of 31.9x amid material regulatorytailwinds from antitrust litigation and questions about AI strategy leadership. The recent -7.3% quarterly price decline has modestly improved the risk/reward, but not enough to warrant a clear directional call.
key_factors: P/E of 31.9x is at the lower end of its 1-year range (27.2x–40.9x) but still historically elevated, leaving limited valuation cushion for multiple compression.
Q1 2025 revenue of $143.76B grew +15.7% YoY with EPS of $2.84 (+18.3% YoY), supported by stable gross margin of 47.3% and operating margin of 32.4%.
Debt-to-equity of 1.004 with total debt declining $14.1B over four quarters to $88.5B, while equity grew to $88.19B, indicating a strengthening balance sheet.

Ongoing U.S. antitrust lawsuit (2026-03-30) and EU Digital Markets Act pressure, plus a partnership-centric AI strategy (Google Gemini integration) that may cede perceived leadership to proprietary-model competitors.

ITEM 1.    BUSINESS
Introduction
The Sherwin-Williams Company, founded in 1866 and incorporated in Ohio in 1884, is engaged in the development, manufacture, distribution and sale of paint, coatings and related products to professional, industrial, commercial and retail customers primarily in North and South America with additional operations in the Caribbean region, Europe, Asia and Australia. Our principal executive offices are located at 1 Sherwin Way, Cleveland, Ohio 44113-2206, telephone (216) 566-2000. As used in this report, the terms "Sherwin-Williams," "Company," "we", "us" and "our" mean The Sherwin-Williams Company and its consolidated subsidiaries.
Available Information
We make available free of charge on or through our website our Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K and amendments to these reports, as soon as reasonably practicable after we electronically file such material with, or furnish such material to, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). You may access these documents on our Investor Relations website, investors.sherwin.com.
We also make available free of charge on our website our Corporate Governance Guidelines, our Director Independence Standards, our Code of Conduct and the charters of our Audit Committee, our Compensation and Management Development Committee and our Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee. You may access these documents on our Investor Relations website, investors.sherwin.com.
Basis of Reportable Segments
The Company reports its segment information in the same way that management internally organizes its business for assessing performance and making decisions regarding allocation of resources. The Company has three reportable operating segments: Paint Stores Group, Consumer Brands Group and Performance Coatings Group (individually, a Reportable Segment and collectively, the Reportable Segments). The Company reports all other business activities and immaterial operating segments that are not reportable in the Administrative function. For further information about the Reportable Segments, see Note 22 to the consolidated financial statements in Item 8.
Paint Stores Group
Paint Stores Group consisted of 4,853 company-operated specialty paint stores in the United States, Canada and the Caribbean region at December 31, 2025. Each store is engaged in servicing the needs of architectural and industrial paint contractors and do-it-yourself homeowners. These stores market and sell Sherwin-Williams® and other controlled brand architectural paint and coatings, protective and marine products, OEM product finishes and related products. The majority of these products are produced by manufac
…[truncated 14267 chars]…
nd advancing a pipeline of talent with a broad mix of skills, backgrounds and experiences. During 2025, we hired approximately 1,700 professionals through our management trainee program as part of our long-term growth initiatives.
We invest in our people by providing learning and employee networking opportunities to drive retention, development and engagement and help employees excel in their current and future roles. During 2025, our employees collectively completed thousands of hours of online and instructor-led courses across a broad range of categories, including leadership, professional skills, technical skills and compliance. We measure our progress toward creating a culture of excellence that empowers employees to learn, grow and achieve their aspirations by conducting periodic pulse surveys and a global engagement survey, which we conducted in 2025 and expect to conduct every other year. We are focused on using these survey results to drive continued progress with our efforts.
Occupational Health and Safety.
Providing safe and healthy working environments for our employees is a core value. We have a consistent focus on Environmental, Health and Safety excellence that promotes employee health and safety, process safety and occupational health, including evaluation and implementation of preventative measures to reduce workplace injuries and illness. We strive for incident-free workplaces and are continuously seeking to improve the programs that are in place to help keep our employees, customers and communities safe, including by regularly re-evaluating our global management systems, standards and performance measures.
Total Rewards.
We prioritize the fair and consistent treatment of our employees in relation to working conditions, wages, benefits, policies and procedures. The Company's policies and programs are designed to respond to the needs of our employees in a manner that provides a safe, professional, efficient and rewarding workplace. Our total rewards programs are designed to offer competitive compensation, comprehensive benefits and other programs to support employees' growth, both personally and professionally, and the needs and well-being of our employees worldwide.
Over the past few years, we have enhanced certain of the Company's benefits and practices to support the health and well-being of our employees. Our enhanced benefits have included paid sick leave, family leave and voluntary leave of absence policies and programs. In 2025, we introduced a new backup child and elder care benefit and increased the annual discount on medical plan contributions that employees can earn by participating in our well-being program. More recently, in 2026 we launched a new global well-being program and platform to give all employees access to engaging tools and resources that will help drive better health outcomes.
Regulatory Compliance
For further information regarding environmental-related matters, see Notes 1, 10 and 19 to the consolidated financial statements in Item 8.

*   **Margins:** Q4 2025 gross margin is 48.4% ($2.71B/$5.60B). Q4 2025 operating margin is 11.4% ($639M/$5.60B). TTM net margin is 10.9%.
*   **Cash-Flow:** No cash flow data supplied.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity increased from $4.05B (Q4 2024) to $4.60B (Q4 2025). Total debt is $9.67B (Q4 2025), relatively flat vs. $9.23B (Q4 2024). Total assets are $25.90B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** YoY EPS growth is marginal at +1.1%. Net income decreased -0.7% YoY. Margins are stable but the sharp QoQ EPS decline (-42.7%) indicates significant quarter-to-quarter volatility.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** High leverage is evident with debt-to-equity of 2.103. Total liabilities of $21.30B exceed total equity by 4.6x.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
*   Current PE (diluted) of 31.243 is within its 1-year range of 29.6 to 37.1.
*   Current price-to-book of 17.456 is at the low end of its 1-year range of 16.5 to 22.2.
*   Earnings yield is 3.2%.
*   ROE has declined from 0.662 (April 2025) to 0.559 (March 2026).

**4. Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
*   Price on 2026-03-31 is $320.55.
*   The stock is -5.7% over the past year and -1.4% over the past quarter.
*   The 52-week high is $379.65; low is $301.58.
*   The recent price decline has occurred alongside stable (though flat YoY) earnings, compressing valuation multiples (PE from ~37 to ~31 over the window).

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Mean reversion in valuation; price-to-book is near the bottom of its 1-year range. ROE remains high at 55.9%, which could support valuation if earnings stabilize.
*   **Downside:** High leverage (D/E 2.103) poses risk in a rising rate or earnings downturn scenario. The sharp QoQ earnings volatility and declining YoY ROE (from 0.662 to 0.559) challenge the durability of current margins and returns.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The stock trades at a moderate historical PE (31.2) with high leverage. Earnings are stable on a TTM basis but exhibit significant quarterly volatility and negligible YoY growth. Price weakness over the past year has brought the valuation to the lower end of its recent range, but this must be weighed against the elevated balance sheet risk and the lack of demonstrated earnings momentum. The quantitative risk/reward appears neutral to marginally negative, contingent on future earnings stability.
SHW.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: The Sherwin-Williams Company (SHW)**
**Date of Assessment:** 2026-04-01 (Based on evidence strictly prior to this date)

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Sherwin-Williams is a global leader in the development, manufacture, distribution, and sale of paint, coatings, and related products. Its business is organized into three integrated segments:
*   **Paint Stores Group:** A network of 4,853 company-operated stores (as of 2025-12-31) in the U.S., Canada, and the Caribbean serving professional contractors and DIY customers. This segment markets and sells primarily Sherwin-Williams® branded products, most of which are sourced from its own Consumer Brands Group, indicating a vertically integrated model (10-K, 2026-02-19).
*   **Consumer Brands Group:** Manufactures and distributes branded and private-label paints (e.g., Valspar®, Dutch Boy®) to retailers, home centers, and dealers. It also operates 307 stores in Latin America. Approximately 63% of its 2025 sales were intersegment transfers to the Paint Stores Group, highlighting operational synergy (10-K, 2026-02-19).
*   **Performance Coatings Group:** Sells industrial, automotive refinish, protective, and marine coatings globally through its own branches and other distributors (10-K, 2026-02-19).

The franchise is built on strong brand recognition, a vast integrated store and manufacturing network, and a diverse product portfolio serving multiple end markets.

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (Dated)
*   **Capital Allocation & Shareholder Return (2026-02-18):** The company declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.80 per common share, marking the 47th consecutive year of dividend increases.
*   **Executive Outlook & Market Catalyst (2026-02-11):** CEO Heidi Petz identified a 6% mortgage rate as a "magic number" that could unlock pent-up housing demand, a key driver for its products. The stock was noted to have rallied 12% year-to-date as rates approached this threshold.
*   **Growth Initiatives & Competition (2026-03-10):** Analysis suggests the company is making heightened investments in customer-facing growth initiatives during a period of competitor retrenchment, which could accelerate share gains with professional contractors.
*   **Operational Outlook (2026-03-03):** The company intends to open 80 to 100 new stores in 2026, leveraging its industry leadership.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks (Inferred from 10-K Cautionary Statements, 2026-02-19):**
*   **Economic Sensitivity:** The business is exposed to general economic conditions, inflation, interest rates, and shifts in consumer behavior during downturns, particularly in cyclical construction markets.
*   **Input Costs & Supply Chain:** Profitability is vulnerable to shortages and cost increases for raw materials (e.g., petrochemical-derived resins and pigments) and energy.
*   **Competitive & Execution Risk:** The company faces intense competition across all segments from numerous local, regional, national, and international players. Failure to keep pace with developments or integrate acquisitions poses a risk.
*   **Regulatory & Litigation:** The company is subject to complex environmental laws and regulations, and faces potential liability from environmental remediation and lead pigment/lead-based paint litigation.

**Offsets (Inferred from 10-K and News):**
*   **Operational Resilience:** The vertically integrated model (own manufacturing serving own stores) provides supply chain control and operational synergies.
*   **Market Position & Brand Strength:** The company holds leading market positions supported by a portfolio of strong, recognized trademarks (10-K, 2026-02-19).
*   **Strategic Investment:** Evidence of continued investment in growth initiatives and store expansion during industry downturns positions the company for potential market share gains (Web context, 2026-03-10).
*   **Financial Strength:** The consistent dividend increase streak suggests stable cash flow generation (News, 2026-02-18).

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral-to-Positive.** The franchise is fundamentally strong, with a dominant integrated retail model and global brand portfolio. Recent narrative points to proactive management investing through industry softness to capture share, supported by a potential near-term catalyst in easing mortgage rates. However, significant macroeconomic, input cost, and competitive risks permeate the outlook, tempering the positive momentum. The assessment is conditioned on the execution of stated growth plans and the resolution of broader economic headwinds.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Sherwin-Williams is a high-quality franchise with a dominant integrated store model and 47 consecutive years of dividend increases, but near-term fundamentals are mixed: earnings growth is negligible (+1.1% YoY EPS), leverage remains elevated (D/E 2.103), and the stock has declined -5.7% over the past year despite stable TTM earnings. Valuation at a P/E of 31.2 and P/B of 17.5 sits at the lower end of its 1-year range, offering modest support, but insufficient earnings momentum and balance sheet risk limit upside conviction.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Sherwin-Williams is a high-quality franchise with a dominant integrated store model and 47 consecutive years of dividend increases, but near-term fundamentals are mixed: earnings growth is negligible (+1.1% YoY EPS), leverage remains elevated (D/E 2.103), and the stock has declined -5.7% over the past year despite stable TTM earnings. Valuation at a P/E of 31.2 and P/B of 17.5 sits at the lower end of its 1-year range, offering modest support, but insufficient earnings momentum and balance sheet risk limit upside conviction.

Earnings momentum is weak: Q4 2025 EPS of $1.92 grew only +1.1% YoY while declining -42.7% QoQ, and ROE has deteriorated from 0.662 to 0.559 over the past year, signaling margin and return pressure.
High leverage persists with debt-to-equity of 2.103 and total liabilities of $21.3B exceeding equity by 4.6x, creating downside risk in a rising rate or earnings downturn scenario.

Potential housing recovery catalyst: CEO identified 6% mortgage rates as a 'magic number' to unlock pent-up demand (2026-02-11), and the company plans 80-100 new store openings in 2026, positioning for share gains during competitor retrenchment.

MCD.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: McDonald's (MCD)**  
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
McDonald's operates a franchise-centric business model, owning and leasing real estate to its franchisees, which provides a stable income stream. The company is characterized by its global scale, strong brand loyalty (ranked as America's favorite fast-food restaurant as of 2026-03-21), and a focus on digital transformation, value pricing, and core menu items. It maintains a 49-year consecutive dividend increase streak, with an expected annual dividend growth rate of 9% (2026-03-24). Its strategy targets 250 million active loyalty program users and $45 billion in annual systemwide sales by end of 2027 (2026-03-05).

### 2. Recent Developments
- **Capital Allocation & Financial Performance**: MCD hit a fresh high in early March 2026, achieving record net margins of 27% in 2025 (2026-03-09). Analysts highlight defensive characteristics and earnings stability, with consensus EPS growth estimates of 7-9% year-over-year (2026-02-21, 2026-02-12).
- **Growth Catalysts**: Management is accelerating new unit growth, with a focus on menu and beverage innovation, digital engagement, and value strategies to drive market share gains (2026-02-12).
- **Competitive Position**: MCD outperformed the S&P 500 in early 2026 (up 8.31% YTD as of 2026-03-18) as investors sought stability. It is positioned as a leader in the growing global QSR market (2026-03-05).
- **Corporate Actions & Partnerships**: McDonald's renewed a five-year strategic partnership with Capgemini to deploy scalable digital platforms, supporting its loyalty and sales goals (2026-03-05). It settled a class-action lawsuit over improper COBRA notices (2026-03-31).
- **Competitive Environment**: Domestic Chinese QSR brands are expanding rapidly, posing a challenge to Western chains' growth in that market (2026-03-04).

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
- **Litigation**: McDonald's faces ongoing threats from obesity-related litigation (2026-03-19) and potential liability from slip-and-fall negligence claims (2026-02-11). It is also involved in an antitrust suit accusing meat processors of fixing pork prices, which could affect input costs (2026-02-26).
- **Market Competition**: Intense competition from value-focused rivals and the rise of localized, agile chains in key growth markets like China could pressure sales (2026-03-09, 2026-03-04).

**Offsets:**
- **Franchise Model**: The asset-light franchise system mitigates direct operational risk and provides contractual cash flow stability.
- **Brand & Scale**: Dominant brand loyalty and global footprint provide pricing power and resilience during economic uncertainty (2026-03-18, 2026-03-21).
- **Strategic Execution**: Ongoing digital transformation and value menu initiatives are clear, actionable levers to strengthen customer engagement and drive sales growth (2026-02-12, 2026-03-05).

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.** The supplied evidence portrays McDonald's as a financially resilient, high-margin franchise with a clear and executable growth strategy centered on digital engagement and value. Its defensive characteristics and consistent shareholder returns are particularly emphasized in the current market context. While litigation and competitive pressures are noted risks, they appear mitigated by the strength of its business model and brand.

*All assessments are based solely on the provided, dated textual evidence. Inferences about resilience are drawn from the described attributes and market performance cited.*

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: McDonald's offers a defensive franchise with best-in-class operating margins (46.1%) and steady earnings growth (+8.2% YoY), but the risk-reward is only marginally positive given persistent negative equity (-$1.79B), high leverage ($40B debt), and a lack of cash flow data to verify franchise cash generation claims. The stock's modest 1.2% annual return despite solid earnings growth suggests limited near-term catalysts for multiple expansion.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: McDonald's offers a defensive franchise with best-in-class operating margins (46.1%) and steady earnings growth (+8.2% YoY), but the risk-reward is only marginally positive given persistent negative equity (-$1.79B), high leverage ($40B debt), and a lack of cash flow data to verify franchise cash generation claims. The stock's modest 1.2% annual return despite solid earnings growth suggests limited near-term catalysts for multiple expansion.
key_factors: Q4 2025 revenue of $7.01B grew +9.7% YoY with EPS of $3.03 (+8.2% YoY), and TTM net income of $8.56B, reflecting steady but not accelerating earnings momentum.

Operating margin remains best-in-class at 46.1% (TTM) with net margin stable at 31.9%, providing a structural profitability buffer, though the qual report's cited '27% record net margin' conflicts with the quant data showing ~32%.

Balance sheet carries significant risk: negative equity of -$1.79B (improved from -$3.80B YoY) and total debt of $40B, with zero cash flow data available across all 8 quarters to verify franchise cash generation stability.

Stock trades at PE of 26.0x with earnings yield of 3.8%, and price return of +1.2% over 252 days materially lags YoY earnings growth of 8.2%, suggesting the market has not rewarded fundamental improvement and near-term catalysts are limited.

Competition
We operate in a highly competitive environment. A number of our marketed products are indicated for disease areas in which other products or treatments are currently available or are being pursued by our competitors through R&D activities. Additionally, some competitor-marketed products target the same genetic pathways as our recently launched marketed products or product candidates. This competition could impact the pricing and market share of our products. We continue to pursue ways of increasing the value of our medicines through innovations, which can include expanding the disease areas for which our products are indicated and finding new methods to make the delivery or manufacture of our medicines easier and less costly. Such activities can offer important opportunities for differentiation. We plan to continue pursuing innovation efforts to strengthen our competitive position. Such position may be based on, among other things, safety, efficacy, reliability, availability, patient convenience, delivery devices, price, reimbursement, access to and timing of market entry and patent position and expiration.
Certain of the existing patents on our principal products have expired, and we face new and increasing competition, including from biosimilars and generics. A biosimilar is another version of a biological product for which marketing approval is sought or has been obtained based on a demonstration that it is "highly similar" to the original reference product. We have experienced adverse effects from biosimilar competition on our originator product sales. Companies have launched versions of EPOGEN, NEUPOGEN, Neulasta and ENBREL (Canada only) with U.S. ENBREL biosimilars approved but not launched. AMGN.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: AMGN (As of 2026-04-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Demonstrated a clear upward trajectory. TTM revenue is $36.75B. Quarterly revenue grew from $7.45B (Q1 2024) to $9.87B (Q4 2025). QoQ growth for Q4 2025 was +3.2%.
*   **Earnings & Margins:** Net income is volatile. TTM net income is $7.71B. Q4 2025 EPS of $2.46 was down -58.5% QoQ from Q3 2025's $5.93. Gross margin improved from 57.0% (Q1 2024) to 69.8% (Q4 2025). Operating margin was 27.6% in Q4 2025.
*   **Cash Flow:** Explicit data is unavailable for all reported periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** High leverage is a defining feature. Total debt is $54.60B against total equity of $8.66B, resulting in a debt-to-equity ratio of 6.307. Total assets are $90.59B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Volatility is high; EPS swung from -$0.21 (Q1 2024) to $5.93 (Q3 2025). The latest quarter's $2.46 EPS represents a significant QoQ decline, indicating potential lumpiness or non-recurring items. The net margin improved to 21% as of 2025-12-31 from 13% a year prior.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The capital structure is highly leveraged (debt-to-equity of 6.307). Resilience is limited by this high debt load relative to the equity base of $8.66B.

**3. Valuation versus History**
*   The latest PE diluted ratio is 24.73 (2026-03-31), sitting at the low end of its 1-year range (24.4 - 49.2). This is a substantial discount to its recent history, where it traded at 35-45x for most of the period between 2025-04-01 and 2026-01-26.
*   The Price-to-Book ratio is 22.03, within its 1-year range of 18.9 - 27.7.
*   Valuation has compressed meaningfully alongside the improvement in reported net margin (from 13% to 21%).

**4. Price Context vs. Fundamentals**
*   **Price Level:** $351.85 (2026-03-31). The 1-year high/low range is $391.29/$261.43.
*   **Returns:** The quarterly return from 2025-12-29 is +6.7%. The annual return from 2025-03-28 is +14.6%.
*   **Context:** The stock's price gain of +14.6% over the past year occurred alongside revenue growth and improved margins, but also through a period of high EPS volatility and elevated leverage. The recent valuation multiple contraction (from ~45x to ~25x) suggests the market may be pricing in the fundamental volatility and balance sheet risk.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** The primary quantitative upside is **valuation compression**. The PE ratio of 24.73 is near the bottom of its annual range and represents a significant de-rating from its prior trading history, potentially offering a value entry if the improved margin trajectory proves durable.
*   **Downside:** The primary quantitative downside is **leverage and earnings volatility**. The debt-to-equity ratio of 6.307 creates significant financial risk. The latest quarter's EPS decline of -58.5% QoQ highlights the instability of earnings, which can exacerbate volatility in a highly leveraged structure.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative profile presents a classic tension. On one side, the stock trades at a historically low valuation multiple (PE 24.7x) alongside improving revenue and margins. On the other, the balance sheet is exceptionally leveraged (Debt/Equity 6.3x) and earnings are highly volatile, with the most recent quarter showing a sharp sequential decline. The reward hinges on the sustainability of margin improvement; the risk is that leverage magnifies any fundamental disappointment. The recent price return (+14.6% annually) has not fully reflected the valuation compression, suggesting the market has shifted its focus toward fundamental risks.
AMGN.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Research Report: Amgen Inc. (AMGN)
**Decision Date:** 2026-04-01
**Evidence Date Range:** 2026-02-10 to 2026-03-31

#### 1. Business/Franchise Description (from Filing Dated 2026-02-13)
Amgen is a global biotechnology company operating in a single segment: human therapeutics. It discovers, develops, manufactures, and delivers innovative medicines, focusing on areas of high unmet medical need. The company markets a portfolio of products across major therapeutic areas including bone health, cardiovascular, inflammation, oncology, and nephrology. Key marketed products include Repatha, Prolia/XGEVA, EVENITY, TEPEZZA, and UPLIZNA. Its business model relies on a combination of direct sales forces and collaborations, with a significant portion of revenues (77% in 2025) concentrated with three major U.S. wholesalers. The company maintains a global presence, with the United States accounting for 73% of 2025 sales.

#### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (Dated Evidence)
*   **Regulatory/Safety (2026-03-31):** The FDA issued a warning about serious, potentially fatal liver injuries linked to Tavneos (avacopan), including 76 postmarketing cases of drug-induced liver injury with 8 deaths.
*   **Competitive Landscape (2026-03-30):** The FDA approved Teva's PONLIMSI, a biosimilar to Amgen's Prolia, increasing biosimilar competition for this key franchise.
*   **Pricing & Access (2026-03-16):** Amgen agreed to join TrumpRx.gov, offering discounts of up to 80% on drugs like Amjevita, in response to policy pressure.
*   **Pipeline Catalysts (2026-03-08 & 10-K):** Progress on the obesity candidate MariTide includes completed Phase 2 studies (January 2026) showing sustained weight loss and tolerability, with multiple Phase 3 trials underway as of November 2025.
*   **Corporate Actions (10-K, 2026-02-13):** Amgen terminated its collaboration for rocatinlimab, transitioning the program to Kyowa Kirin in the first quarter of 2026. Amgen will continue manufacturing for a transitional period.
*   **Regulatory Challenge (10-K, 2026-02-13):** The FDA requested voluntary withdrawal of Tavneos in January 2026 due to clinical trial process concerns and hepatotoxicity risk. Amgen refused and is evaluating next steps with the FDA.
*   **Pipeline Setback (10-K, 2026-02-13):** Based on Phase 3 data, Amgen decided not to pursue regulatory approval for bemarituzumab in first-line gastric cancer (February 2026).

#### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Safety & Regulatory Risk (2026-03-31, 10-K):** The FDA's safety warning and prior withdrawal request for Tavneos create significant commercial and regulatory uncertainty for this product.
*   **Biosimilar Erosion (2026-03-30, 10-K):** The arrival of biosimilars for key legacy products (Prolia/XGEVA patents expired in 2025) poses a direct threat to revenue streams. The filing explicitly states that biosimilar competition has previously impacted sales.
*   **Patent Cliff:** The filing details expiration dates for core product patents, with some (e.g., EVENITY U.S. antibody patent, April 2026) expiring near-term, exposing them to generic/biosimilar entry.
*   **Pipeline Execution Risk (2026-02-13):** Recent clinical setbacks (bemarituzumab) and strategic exits (rocatinlimab) highlight the inherent risk and high cost of drug development.

**Offsets:**
*   **Pipeline Catalysts (10-K, 2026-03-08):** MariTide represents a major late-stage growth opportunity in obesity/weight management with differentiated (once-monthly) dosing. Multiple Phase 3 trials across different indications are underway.
*   **Portfolio Diversification & New Indications (10-K):** Success in expanding labels (e.g., Repatha to broader CV risk, UPLIZNA to new diseases, TEZSPIRE to nasal polyps) and new product approvals (IMDELLTRA full approval) demonstrate the value of the existing portfolio.
*   **Patent Protection (10-K):** The company holds long-dated patents on key products (e.g., Enbrel, TEPEZZA, LUMAKRAS), providing a buffer against competition for several years on select franchises.

#### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.** The franchise demonstrates stability through its established portfolio and significant long-term patent protection on key assets. However, near-term headwinds are pronounced: the acute regulatory challenge for Tavneos, the imminent biosimilar erosion of Prolia/XGEVA, and recent clinical setbacks partially offset the promising, but still unproven, late-stage opportunity in MariTide. The balance between tangible near-term risks and transformative long-term pipeline potential currently presents a neutral qualitative outlook.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Gross margin improved from 57.0% (Q1 2024) to 69.8% (Q4 2025).' The raw income statement shows Q1 2024 gross profit of $4.25B on revenue of $7.45B = 57.1% (close enough), and Q4 2025 gross profit of $6.89B on revenue of $9.87B = 69.8%. These are consistent. However, the report also states 'Operating margin was 27.6% in Q4 2025' while the raw data shows operating income of $2.72B / revenue of $9.87B = 27.6%. This is consistent. No correction needed on these specific figures.
corrections.required_action: No action needed — figures verified against raw data.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'The net margin improved to 21% as of 2025-12-31 from 13% a year prior.' The raw valuation ratios confirm net_margin=0.21 at 2026-02-26 and 2026-03-31, and net_margin=0.13 at 2025-04-01 through 2026-01-26. However, the raw income statement shows Q4 2025 net income of $1.33B on revenue of $9.87B = 13.5%, not 21%. The 21% figure appears to be a TTM net margin (TTM net income $7.71B / TTM revenue $36.75B = 21.0%). The report conflates quarterly and TTM margins without clarifying which period the 21% refers to. The valuation ratio panel's net_margin=0.21 is TTM-based, not quarterly.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the 21% net margin is TTM-based, not the Q4 2025 quarterly margin (which is ~13.5%). This distinction matters because the Q4 quarterly margin is much lower than the TTM figure, reinforcing the earnings volatility concern.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Valuation has compressed meaningfully alongside the improvement in reported net margin (from 13% to 21%)' implying a causal or directional link. The raw valuation history shows PE dropped from ~44.8x (2026-01-26) to ~24.7x (2026-03-31) — a ~45% compression in roughly two months. This coincides with the net_margin stepping from 0.13 to 0.21 in the valuation panel, which is a TTM rolling update (likely when Q4 2025 earnings were incorporated into the TTM denominator). The PE compression is therefore partly mechanical (higher TTM earnings denominator) and partly reflects genuine price decline (price went from ~$350 range to $351.85, roughly flat, while TTM EPS rose). The report's framing that 'the market may be pricing in fundamental volatility' is speculative and not directly supported by the data — the PE drop is largely an earnings-roll artifact.
corrections.required_action: Reframe the PE compression as largely mechanical (TTM earnings update) rather than implying market re-rating due to fundamental concerns. The stock price was roughly flat over the period of PE compression.

unsupported_claims: : The quant report's statement 'The recent price return (+14.6% annually) has not fully reflected the valuation compression, suggesting the market has shifted its focus toward fundamental risks' is internally contradictory. If the stock price rose 14.6% while PE compressed from ~45x to ~25x, this means TTM earnings grew faster than the price — the market did not 'shift focus toward fundamental risks' but rather earnings simply caught up to the price. This narrative framing is unsupported by the raw data.

: The qual report states 'Prolia/XGEVA patents expired in 2025' as a risk. The 10-K confirms 'Our patents for RANKL antibodies, including sequences, for Prolia expired in February 2025 in the United States and in November 2025 in select countries in Europe.' This is verified. However, the qual report does not quantify the revenue impact of this expiration, and the raw data does not include product-level revenue breakdowns in the quantitative pack, so the magnitude of this risk cannot be assessed from the supplied evidence alone.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Amgen presents a balanced risk/reward profile: genuine revenue momentum (TTM $36.75B, +12.4% YoY) and meaningful debt reduction ($64.6B to $54.6B) are offset by extreme financial leverage (D/E 6.3x), confirmed patent cliffs (Prolia/XGEVA biosimilar entry March 2026), and acute regulatory risk (Tavneos FDA withdrawal request). The stock's PE compression from ~45x to ~25x is largely mechanical (TTM earnings roll) rather than a market re-rating, leaving the stock fairly valued at current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Amgen presents a balanced risk/reward profile: genuine revenue momentum (TTM $36.75B, +12.4% YoY) and meaningful debt reduction ($64.6B to $54.6B) are offset by extreme financial leverage (D/E 6.3x), confirmed patent cliffs (Prolia/XGEVA biosimilar entry March 2026), and acute regulatory risk (Tavneos FDA withdrawal request). The stock's PE compression from ~45x to ~25x is largely mechanical (TTM earnings roll) rather than a market re-rating, leaving the stock fairly valued at current levels.
key_factors: Revenue growth remains genuine: TTM revenue of $36.75B with Q4 2025 quarterly revenue of $9.87B (+3.2% QoQ), and gross margin improved from 57.0% (Q1 2024) to 69.8% (Q4 2025), demonstrating operational leverage.

Balance sheet deleveraging is real but leverage remains extreme: total debt decreased from $64.61B to $54.60B over two years, yet debt-to-equity of 6.307 ($54.6B debt vs $8.66B equity) creates significant financial risk if earnings disappoint.

Patent cliff materializing: FDA approved Teva's PONLIMSI biosimilar to Prolia (March 2026), and EVENITY U.S. antibody patent expires April 2026, creating near-term revenue headwinds for legacy franchises.

Pipeline optionality via MariTide: Phase 2 data (January 2026) showed sustained weight loss with once-monthly dosing; multiple Phase 3 trials underway across obesity/weight management indications, representing a transformative but unproven growth opportunity.

5
Table of Contents
Competitive Environment
Caterpillar products and services are sold worldwide into a variety of highly competitive markets.  In all markets, we compete on the basis of product performance, customer service, quality and price.  From time to time, the intensity of competition results in price discounting in a particular industry or region.  Such price discounting puts pressure on margins and can negatively impact operating profit. Outside the United States, certain competitors enjoy competitive advantages inherent to operating in their home countries or regions.
Raw Materials and Component Products
We source our raw materials and manufactured components from suppliers both domestically and internationally. These purchases include unformed materials and rough and finished parts.  Unformed materials include a variety of steel products, which are then cut or formed to shape and machined in our facilities. Rough parts include various sized steel and iron castings and forgings, which are machined to final specification levels inside our facilities. Finished parts are ready to assemble components, which are made either to Caterpillar specifications or to supplier developed specifications.  We machine and assemble some of the components used in our machines, engines and power generation units and to support our after-market dealer parts sales. We also purchase various goods and services used in production, logistics, offices and product development processes.  We maintain global strategic sourcing models to meet our global facilities' production needs while building long-term supplier relationships and leveraging enterprise spend.  We expect our suppliers to maintain, at all times, industry-leading levels of quality and the ability to timely deliver raw materials and component products for our machine and engine products. However, in some cases, increases in demand or supply chain disruptions have led to parts and components constraints across some products. We use a variety of agreements with suppliers to protect our intellectual property and processes to monitor and mitigate risks of the supply base causing a business disruption.  The risks monitored include supplier financial viability, the ability to increase or decrease production levels, business continuity, quality and delivery.
Patents and Trademarks
We own a number of patents and trademarks, which have been obtained over a period of years and relate to the products we manufacture and the services we provide. These patents and trademarks are generally considered beneficial to our business. We do not regard our business as being dependent upon any single patent or group of patents.
Order Backlog
The dollar amount of backlog believed to be firm was approximately $51.2 billion at December 31, 2025 and $30.0 billion at December 31, 2024. Compared with year-end 2024, the order backlog increased across the primary segments, with the largest increase in Power & Energy. Of the total backlog at December 31, 2025, approximately $19.3 billion was not expected to be filled in 2026.
Dealers and Distributors
We distribute our machines principally through a worldwide organization of dealers (dealer network), 41 located in the United States and 109 located outside the United States, serving 190 countries.  We sell reciprocating engines principally through the dealer network and to other manufacturers for use in products. We also sell some of the reciprocating engines manufactured by our subsidiary Perkins Engines Company Limited through its worldwide network of 86 distributors covering 183 countries.  We sell the FG Wilson branded electric power generation systems through its worldwide network of 108 distributors covering 159 countries.
Our dealers do not deal exclusively with our products; however, in most cases sales and servicing of our products are the dealers' principal business.  We sell some products, primarily turbines and locomotives, directly to end customers through sales forces employed by the company.  At times, these employees are assisted by independent sales representatives.
While the large majority of our worldwide dealers are independently owned and operated, we own and operate a dealership in Japan that covers approximately 80% of the Japanese market:  Nippon Caterpillar Division.  We are currently operating this Japanese dealer directly and we report its results in the All Other Segment.  There are also three independent dealers in the Southern Region of Japan.

Pursuant to these agreements, the company grants the dealer the right to purchase and sell its products and to service the products in a specified geographic service territory.  The company establishes prices to dealers after receiving input from dealers on transactional pricing in the marketplace.  The company also agrees to defend its intellectual property and to provide warranty and technical support to the dealer. CAT.quantitative_report:
### Quantitative Report: CAT (Decision Date: 2026-04-01)

**1. Financial Trajectory (Q4 2025)**
- **Revenue & Earnings:** Strong revenue trajectory with QoQ growth of +8.5% (Q4 2025: 19.13B vs Q3: 17.64B) and YoY growth of +18.0%. However, earnings are deteriorating: YoY EPS diluted is -11.4% (Q4 2025: 5.12 vs Q4 2024: 5.78), and YoY net income is -13.9%.
- **Margins:** Margin compression is evident. Gross margin (gross profit/revenue) in Q4 2025 is 30.5% (5.83B/19.13B), down from 36.3% in Q4 2024 (5.89B/16.21B). Operating margin fell to 13.9% (2.66B/19.13B) from 18.0% (2.92B/16.21B) YoY.
- **Cash Flow:** Unavailable. No cash flow data is supplied.
- **Balance Sheet:** Assets and liabilities have grown. Total assets increased to 98.58B from 87.76B at the end of 2024, and total liabilities rose to 77.27B from 68.27B, increasing leverage (total liabilities/equity from 3.50x to 3.62x). Equity grew to 21.32B from 19.49B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Weakening. The divergence between strong revenue growth (+18.0% YoY) and contracting earnings (-13.9% YoY) indicates declining earnings quality, driven by cost increases (cost of revenue up YoY) and margin erosion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Leverage is high and rising. The latest debt-to-equity ratio (total liabilities/total equity) is 3.62x (77.27B/21.32B). While equity grew, the increase in liabilities outpaced it, indicating reduced financial flexibility.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- The stock is trading at historically high multiples. The latest PE diluted of 37.664 is near the top of its 365-day range (12.4 - 41.2). The P/B ratio of 15.696 is also near its high (range: 6.9 - 20.5).
- This valuation expansion has occurred alongside fundamental deterioration. Net margin has compressed from 0.166 (April 2025) to 0.131 (Feb/Mar 2026), and ROE has fallen from 0.553 to 0.417. The stock's multiple has expanded despite these declines in profitability.

**4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals**
- **Price Level:** The latest close is 708.46 (2026-03-31), near the high of 789.81 for the period.
- **Returns:** The stock has experienced massive momentum: a +22.4% quarterly return (from 578.61) and a +114.9% annual return (from 329.69).
- **Disconnect:** The price surge contrasts sharply with the fundamental path of declining margins and earnings. The rally appears driven by valuation multiple expansion rather than improved fundamental metrics.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** Continued revenue growth momentum (QoQ: +8.5%, YoY: +18.0%) could eventually translate into improved earnings if cost pressures subside. The market is currently rewarding revenue scale with a high premium.
- **Downside:** The valuation is extremely stretched relative to current profitability. A reversion of the PE multiple toward its historical average (around 15-20x) combined with continued margin pressure poses significant downside risk. The high leverage (liabilities/equity of 3.62x) could amplify financial stress if earnings do not improve.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The risk/reward profile appears unfavorable based on the supplied numbers. The stock is priced for perfection, trading at peak valuations (PE 37.7x) while its fundamental earnings quality deteriorates (margins compressing, ROE declining). The +115% annual price return has created a significant gap between valuation and current profitability (net margin 13.1%, declining EPS). The absence of cash flow data prevents a full assessment of cash generation, but the balance sheet shows rising leverage. A quantitative reversion towards historical valuation norms poses material downside risk, while further upside requires a reversal of negative earnings trends not yet evidenced in the data.
CAT.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Analysis: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT)
**Evidence Date:** Point-in-time data through 2026-03-31.

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2026-02-13 10-K)**
Caterpillar is the world's leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, engines, turbines, and locomotives. It operates across three primary segments: **Construction Industries**, **Resource Industries**, and **Power & Energy**, supported by a large global dealer network (150 independent dealers across 190 countries) and **Cat Financial**, its captive financing arm. The company competes on performance, quality, service, and price in highly competitive global markets against major players like Deere, Komatsu, and Cummins. Its enterprise strategy, refreshed in 2025, focuses on commercial excellence, advanced technology leadership, and operational transformation.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated Events from Supplied Text)**
*   **AI & Infrastructure Catalyst (2026-03-23, 2026-03-08, 2026-03-07):** Caterpillar is increasingly framed as an indirect beneficiary of AI-driven data center construction and power demand. A specific $840 million agreement with Atlas Energy Solutions to build 2 GW of generation capacity was highlighted (2026-03-23). Articles cite data center construction, reshoring of U.S. manufacturing, and backup power as key 2026 catalysts (2026-03-08).
*   **Geopolitical Sensitivity & Commodity Links (2026-03-19, 2026-03-23, 2026-03-25):** The stock's performance is tied to broader geopolitical and energy markets. It declined on 2026-03-19 amid an oil price spike due to Iran tensions. It is cited as a beneficiary of potential U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks and lower oil prices (2026-03-25).
*   **Market Backdrop & Valuation (2026-03-08, 2026-03-01):** Reports note the stock's strong momentum (up 124% over one year as of 2026-03-01) but caution that its 37x P/E ratio is significantly above its historical average (2026-03-08).
*   **Patent Litigation (2026-02-24, 2026-03-25):** A dispute with Doosan Bobcat has escalated. The U.S. International Trade Commission opened an investigation into Caterpillar's complaint in early 2026 (2026-02-24). Caterpillar has countersued Bobcat, alleging Bobcat's actions could undermine its business operations (2026-03-25).
*   **Tariff Risk (2026-03-15):** A proposed 15% global tariff by President Trump is flagged as a risk for manufacturers reliant on imported materials, a category that includes Caterpillar.
*   **Industry Outlook (2026-03-16):** The global heavy construction equipment market is projected to grow at a 5.0% CAGR through 2030, with mining as the fastest-growing segment.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Geopolitical/Energy Volatility:** Performance is exposed to oil price shocks and geopolitical events (e.g., Iran tensions) that can disrupt markets, as seen on 2026-03-19.
    *   **Elevated Valuation:** The stock's 37x P/E ratio is noted as significantly above its five-year average (2026-03-08), posing a potential headwind if growth expectations are not met.
    *   **Tariff Exposure:** Proposed global tariffs could increase costs for import-reliant manufacturers (2026-03-15).
    *   **Legal/IP Disputes:** The escalating patent fight with Doosan Bobcat presents operational and legal risk, though Caterpillar is actively challenging the claims. Regulatory compliance costs are also noted as ongoing (10-K).
    *   **Cyclicality & Competition:** The business is cyclical and operates in highly competitive markets with global and regional players, which can pressure margins (10-K).

*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Secular Growth Drivers:** The company is positioned to benefit from multiple secular trends: AI data center construction, infrastructure investment, reshoring of U.S. manufacturing, and mining for energy transition commodities (2026-03-08, 2026-03-07, 2026-03-01).
    *   **Large Backlog & Financial Strength:** Demonstrated by a substantial $51.2 billion firm backlog at end-2025, an A+ credit rating, and a history of dividend increases (10-K, 2026-03-01).
    *   **Strategic Partnerships:** Partnerships like those with Atlas Energy (2026-03-23) and Vertiv position it to capture emerging opportunities in power infrastructure.
    *   **Diversified Portfolio:** Operations span construction, resources, and energy/power, mitigating single-market dependency.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive**, balanced by near-term headwinds. The franchise is fundamentally strong, with clear leadership in essential industrial equipment and large, diversified end markets. The narrative is increasingly positive due to tangible catalysts in AI infrastructure, reshoring, and energy demand, supported by a robust balance sheet and backlog. However, this is counterbalanced by material near-term risks including potential tariff impacts, a legally contentious environment, sensitivity to geopolitical energy shocks, and a stock valuation that has priced in significant future growth. The qualitative thesis hinges on the realization of long-term secular drivers versus the potential for cyclical and regulatory headwinds.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: CAT is a high-quality industrial franchise benefiting from powerful secular tailwinds in AI/data center power and infrastructure, but the stock has priced in years of future growth at a 37.7x P/E while near-term fundamentals are actively deteriorating—margins have compressed for eight consecutive quarters, EPS is declining, and leverage is rising. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: the narrative floor is real but the valuation leaves no margin of safety.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: CAT is a high-quality industrial franchise benefiting from powerful secular tailwinds in AI/data center power and infrastructure, but the stock has priced in years of future growth at a 37.7x P/E while near-term fundamentals are actively deteriorating—margins have compressed for eight consecutive quarters, EPS is declining, and leverage is rising. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: the narrative floor is real but the valuation leaves no margin of safety.
key_factors: Valuation at cycle extremes: PE diluted of 37.7x is near the top of its 365-day range (12.4–41.2), P/B of 15.7x near its high (6.9–20.5), pricing in perfection while net margin has compressed from 16.6% (Apr 2025) to 13.1% (Feb/Mar 2026) and ROE has fallen from 55.3% to 41.7%.

Fundamental deterioration despite revenue growth: YoY revenue grew +18.0% but EPS diluted fell -11.4% and net income declined -13.9%, with gross margin dropping from 36.3% to 30.5% and operating margin from 18.0% to 13.9% YoY—indicating severe cost pressure eroding earnings quality.

Secular catalysts provide a narrative floor: a $51.2B firm backlog, a specific $840M Atlas Energy deal for 2 GW of generation capacity (2026-03-23), and positioning for AI data center construction, reshoring, and energy transition create durable demand visibility.

Rising leverage and geopolitical/tariff risks: total liabilities/equity increased from 3.50x to 3.62x, a proposed 15% global tariff (2026-03-15) could raise input costs, and oil price sensitivity was demonstrated by a decline on 2026-03-19 amid Iran tensions.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2026-02-13 section=business period_end=2025-12-31
Item 1. Business.
Overview
JPMorgan Chase & Co. ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm", NYSE: JPM), a financial holding company incorporated under Delaware law in 1968, is a leading financial services firm based in the United States of America ("U.S."), with operations worldwide. JPMorganChase had $4.4 trillion in assets and $362.4 billion in stockholders' equity as of December 31, 2025. The Firm is a leader in investment banking, financial services for consumers and small businesses, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management. Under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands, the Firm serves millions of customers, predominantly in the U.S., and many of the world's most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients globally.
JPMorganChase's principal bank subsidiary is JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association ("JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A."), a national banking association with U.S. branches in 48 states and Washington, D.C. JPMorganChase's principal non-bank subsidiary is J.P. Morgan Securities LLC ("J.P. Morgan Securities"), a U.S. broker-dealer. The bank and non-bank subsidiaries of JPMorganChase operate nationally as well as through overseas branches and subsidiaries, representative offices and subsidiary foreign banks. The Firm's principal operating subsidiaries outside the U.S. are J.P. Morgan Securities plc and J.P. Morgan SE ("JPMSE"), which are subsidiaries of JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. and are based in the United Kingdom ("U.K.") and Germany, respectively.
The Firm's website is www.jpmorganchase.com. JPMorganChase makes available on its website, free of charge, annual reports on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K pursuant to Section 13(a) or Section 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as soon as reasonably practicable after it electronically files or furnishes such material to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") at www.sec.gov. JPMorganChase makes new and important information about the Firm available on its website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com, including on the Investor Relations section of its website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir. Information on the Firm's website, including documents on the website that are referenced in this Form 10-K, is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025 ("2025 Form 10-K" or "Form 10-K") or the Firm's other filings with the SEC.
Business segments & Corporate
For management reporting purposes, the Firm has three reportable business segments – Consumer & Community Banking ("CCB"), Commercial & Investment Bank ("CIB") and Asset & Wealth Management ("AWM") – with the remaining activities in Corporate. The Firm's consumer business segment is CCB, and the Firm's wholesale business segments are CIB and AWM.
A description of the Firm's reportable business segments and the products and services that they provide to their respective client bases, as well as a description of Corporate activities, is provided in the Management's discussion and analysis of financial condition and results of operations section of this Form 10-K ("Management's discussion and analysis" or "MD&A") under the heading "Business Segment & Corporate Results," which begins on page 46, and in Note 32.
Competition
JPMorganChase and its subsidiaries and affiliates operate in highly competitive environments. Competitors include other banks, brokerage firms, investment banking companies, merchant banks, hedge funds, commodity trading companies, private equity firms, insurance companies, mutual fund companies, investment managers, credit card companies, mortgage banking companies, trust companies, securities processing companies, automobile financing companies, leasing companies, e-commerce and other internet-based companies, digital asset and other financial technology companies, and other companies engaged in providing similar and new products and services. The Firm's businesses generally compete on the basis of the quality and variety of the Firm's products and services, transaction execution, innovation, reputation and price. Competition also varies based on the types of clients, customers, industries and geographies served. With respect to some of its geographies and products, JPMorganChase competes globally; with respect to others, the Firm competes on a national or regional basis. New competitors in the financial services industry continue to emerge, including firms that offer products and services solely through the internet and non-financial companies that offer products and services that disintermediate traditional banking products and services offered by financial services firms such as JPMorganChase.
